filter_index.video_title: Sandakphu Phalut - A trek with unforgettable views!

snow_or_rain_notes: Trek closed May to August due to monsoon
Mid-April onwards rains become more frequent, making the trek less enjoyable
Spring showers can cause fog and obscure long-distance peak views
temperature_notes: Winter daytime: 8°C to 12°C; nighttime: 0°C to -3°C; 5 warm layers required
Spring daytime: 15°C to 20°C; nighttime: 1°C to 5°C; 4 warm layers required
Autumn daytime: 13°C to 18°C; nighttime: 5°C to 0°C; 4 warm layers required

terrain_challenges: Uneven trails with daily ascents and descents
Continuous steep ascent from Sepi to Rammam village
Continuous 10 km steep ascent from Samanden to Molley with 4,460 ft height gain
Steep descent from Sandakphu to Gurdum that can get slippery and tricky in rain or snow
Walking on an exposed ridge from Phalut to Sandakphu
altitude_or_weather_risks: Trek reaches up to 11,930 ft with total gain of ~5,530 ft
Day 3 climb from 7,760 ft to 11,220 ft (4,460 ft gain) can contribute to altitude sickness
Risk of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) above 10,000 ft; can affect first-time and experienced trekkers alike
Even fit trekkers climbing too high too soon are at equal risk of AMS
Extremely windy and cold on the ridge from Phalut to Sandakphu, especially in autumn and winter

fitness_benchmark: To do the Sandakphu Phalut Trek comfortably, you must be able to walk, jog or run 5 km in under 35 minutes. This is the minimum recommended fitness level that you must aim for.
approval_requirements: Make sure you upload these screenshots 15 to 20 days before your trek date. We want to see that you are being consistent in your preparation.
Your screenshots must demonstrate that you are meeting the fitness criteria for your trek.
prep_window: Make sure you upload these screenshots 15 to 20 days before your trek date

experience_themes: Spring: Rhododendrons and Magnolias in bloom along the trail
Autumn: Crisp air, clear mountain views, golden colours at sunrise/sunset, bird watching in the national park
Winter: Mild snowfall at Sandakphu and Phalut, colourful twilight skies, sunrise and sunset below the clouds, solitude on the trail
Winter: Some of the best twilight skies; sun rises and sets below the clouds
Spring: Views may be limited by fog and showers, but flowers compensate
Autumn: Clearest air and best long-distance peak views; golden sunrise/sunset colours
primary_watchouts: Mid-April: increasing rain frequency reduces trek enjoyment
October: heavy crowds due to Durga Puja holidays and Land Rover tourists from Bengal
Winter: roads become difficult to drive on, reducing tourist numbers
Mid-to-late April due to frequent rains
October for those seeking solitude, due to Durga Puja crowds
Uneven trails with daily ascents and descents
best_for: Bird watching year-round (described as one of the Best Himalayan treks for Bird Watching)
Experiencing rhododendron and magnolia blooms in spring
Clear long-distance peak views and golden sunrise/sunset colours in autumn
Mild snow experience and solitude in winter
not_ideal_for: Mid-to-late April due to frequent rains
October for those seeking solitude, due to Durga Puja crowds

video_title: Sandakphu Phalut - A trek with unforgettable views!
